description: The decade-long legal battle in the US over the availability of the Plan B emergency contraceptive over-the-counter for minors. It culminated in 2013 with a federal judge ordering the FDA to remove all age and point-of-sale restrictions.

key_events.description: The FDA repeatedly delays decision-making due to political pressure.
Federal Judge Korman calls the administration's actions 'arbitrary and capricious'.
The Obama administration finally drops its appeal against the court order.
Plan B becomes available without age limits on pharmacy shelves.

calibration_notes: Illustrates the collision of political interference and regulatory independence in drug access.
